summary:
The report is written. Here's a one-paragraph summary of what I did.

I compiled the three review critics' verdicts into a single PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. While reconciling the verdicts I found a factual conflict: `guide-alignment-critic` returned `needs_work` (1 major + 3 minor) claiming the human gate-approval path never broadcasts status, while `requirements-conformance-critic` passed and reported that same item fixed. I resolved it against ground truth rather than parroting either verdict — the git timeline shows `guide-alignment-critic (iteration 3)` ran at commit `5bf5563`, *before* `implementer (iteration 4)` (`ac66724`) landed the fixes, and the two other critics ran after. I verified each of the four guide-alignment findings against the committed tree (F1: `approve.rb:35` calls `BroadcastStatus`; F2: controller reads `result.value.completed?` with no reload; F3: `tmp_run_tests.rb` is gone; F4: the partial uses `gray-500`/`gray-900`, no `gray-700`) and confirmed the two backend-atomicity items are in place. The report therefore states an honest overall verdict of **ready to merge (0 open findings)** — covering what was asked (18 requirements), what was built (the `StatusSummary` PORO, `BroadcastStatus` service, shared partial, gate path, tests, with real per-file line counts), an evidence-of-conformance table, a findings-and-resolution table, an explicit note explaining the stale-verdict discrepancy, and a merge recommendation with the one procedural caveat that the guide-alignment verdict on record predates the fixes.
